title: Experienced GoHighLevel Automation & Systems Specialist
description:
I am looking to hire a highly organized, systems-driven Virtual Assistant who already has experience working inside GoHighLevel and preferably REI Reply as well.



This is not a basic VA role. I am looking for someone who truly understands backend systems, automations, workflows, pipelines, integrations, admin setup, and troubleshooting inside GoHighLevel.



I personally love systems, automation, and everything GoHighLevel can do, but I do not have the time to manage and build everything myself. I want someone who enjoys this type of work, thinks systematically, solves problems, and can eventually become the go-to person for helping me organize, build, improve, and manage the backend of my business.



I need someone who can help take ideas like:



“Set up this automation”

“Build this workflow”

“Figure out how to make the AI do this”

“Set up and manage the social media planner”

“Organize this part of the CRM”

“Troubleshoot why this isn’t working”

“Create a system for this process”



…and actually bring them to life inside GoHighLevel or REI Reply.



What I’m looking for



I am looking for someone who is:



already experienced in GoHighLevel

very comfortable with automations, workflows, triggers, pipelines, calendars, forms, websites, funnels, and CRM organization

highly organized and systematic in the way they think

a problem solver who can figure things out

detail-oriented and reliable

able to communicate clearly and professionally

proactive, not someone who needs to be micromanaged

comfortable helping with both setup and ongoing backend support

Main responsibilities



Your responsibilities may include:



Building and updating GoHighLevel workflows and automations

Setting up and managing pipelines, opportunities, tags, custom fields, calendars, forms, and funnels

Troubleshooting automation issues and system errors

Helping organize backend processes so the business runs more efficiently

Assisting with AI setup and automation-related tasks inside the platform

Managing or helping configure social media planner tools inside REI Reply / GoHighLevel

Connecting and testing integrations

Improving existing systems and suggesting better ways to do things

Documenting processes and helping create more structure over time

Preferred experience



Bonus points if you have experience with:



REI Reply

real estate or wholesaling-related CRM systems

GoHighLevel AI tools

social media planner setup inside GHL

Zapier or other integrations

SOP creation and documentation

backend admin support for online businesses

Who this role is for



This role is for someone who genuinely enjoys backend systems and automation work.



If you love cleaning up messy systems, building workflows, organizing CRM structure, solving backend problems, and making businesses run smoother through automation, this may be a great fit.



If you are more of a general VA and do not have strong GoHighLevel experience already, this role is probably not the right fit.

title: Digital Marketing Manager — Trades Business (Growth Role)
description:
THIS ROLE IS FOR SOMEONE WHO WANTS TO OWN SOMETHING — NOT JUST COMPLETE TASKS.



We are Elite Trades Australia. We are a Melbourne-based trades business with 15+ licensed plumbers, electricians, and HVAC technicians completing real jobs across Melbourne every day. Our core speciality is heat pump installations, split system installs, and government-backed VEU energy upgrade programs.



Right now, Melbourne doesn't know who we are. That is about to change.



We are hiring a Digital Marketing Manager to build our brand from the ground up — and eventually own it completely. You will start by working closely with the business owner. Over time, as you prove yourself, this role becomes fully yours to run.



This is not a "post some content and clock out" job. We want someone who thinks, who problem-solves, who looks at our business and asks "what should we be doing that we're not?" If that's you, read on.



---



WHAT THIS ROLE COVERS



You will own our entire digital presence. That includes:



SOCIAL MEDIA

- Run Instagram and
 — strategy, content calendar, 

  posting, engagement, DMs, comments

- Create Reels and short-form video content from raw job footage 

  sent by our field team — before/afters, installs, team culture, 

  educational content about heat pumps and energy savings

- Think beyond just posting — study what is working in the 

  trades and home services space and bring those ideas to us



VIDEO EDITING

- Edit 4–6 short-form videos per week — fast-paced, 

  mobile-first, Reels format

- Add captions, music, motion graphics, and text overlays

- Repurpose long content into short clips across platforms

- Own the visual style of Elite Trades — we want a consistent, 

  professional look that makes us stand out from every other 

  tradie page in Melbourne



GOOGLE AND WEBSITE

- Manage and optimise our Google Business Profile — posts, 

  photos, Q&As, review responses, service updates

- Use Google tools (Search Console, Analytics, Trends) to 

  understand what people are searching for and report back to us

- Coordinate basic website updates — landing page copy, 

  service pages, SEO improvements — working with our web 

  developer or making minor edits yourself

- Monitor our online reputation across Google,
, 

  and any directory listings



REBRANDING ELITE TRADES

- Help us define what Elite Trades looks and sounds like online

- Develop a consistent visual identity — colour palette, 

  tone of voice, post templates, video style

- We want to look like a premium, trustworthy, professional 

  business — not a random tradie with a phone camera

- Bring ideas. We are open to them.



OTHER ADVERTISING AND CHANNELS

- Research and recommend platforms we are not using yet — 

  TikTok, LinkedIn, YouTube Shorts, Houzz, hipages, 

  Google Local Services, etc.

- Set up and manage directory listings (hipages, 

  ServiceSeeking, Oneflare, True Local, Houzz)

- Monitor and report on paid ad performance 

  (we run Google Ads — you track spend and results weekly)

- Look for opportunities we have not thought of yet. 

  Bring them to us.



TOOLS AND EFFICIENCY

- Use AI tools, scheduling platforms (Later, Buffer, or 

  similar), Canva, and any other tools that make you 

  faster and better

- We expect you to know your tools and use them — 

  do not wait to be told what software to use

- Research competitors, study their content, and tell us 

  what is working in the market

- Build systems for yourself so this role scales — 

  templates, content banks, caption libraries, 

  a repeatable weekly workflow



LEADS AND BUSINESS SUPPORT

- Send review requests to customers after completed jobs

- Respond to social media enquiries and DMs 

  (scripts provided, but you will improve them over time)

- Build contact lists of Melbourne property managers 

  and strata companies for B2B outreach

- Track all marketing activity in a simple weekly 

  report — what was posted, what performed, 

  what leads came in, what needs attention



---



WHO WE ARE LOOKING FOR



We are not looking for someone who needs to be told what to do every day.



We are looking for someone who:

- Is a strong video editor first — this skill is non-negotiable

- Understands social media as a business tool, not just content

- Is curious and self-directed — you Google things, 

  you test things, you figure things out

- Communicates clearly and proactively — 

  you update us before we have to ask

- Takes ownership — if something is not working, 

  you say so and suggest a fix

- Wants to grow — this role will expand as the 

  business grows, and so will your responsibilities and pay



You do NOT need to know the trades industry. We will teach you 

everything about our business. What we cannot teach is drive, 

creativity, and the ability to take ownership.



---



MUST HAVES



- Proven video editing portfolio — short-form, 

  Reels/TikTok format, captions, motion text

- Experience managing a real business social media account

- Strong written English

- Comfortable with CapCut, Premiere Pro, or DaVinci Resolve

- Available to overlap with AEST hours — 

  minimum 9am to 5pm Monday to Friday

- Fast internet connection and a reliable computer

- Self-starter — you do not need daily hand-holding



---



WHAT WE OFFER



- $950 AUD per month — full time, 40 hours per week

- Paid monthly

- Performance and pay review at 6 months — 

  this role pays more as you take on more

- Long-term permanent position — we are building 

  something and we want you to build it with us

- Direct access to the business owner — 

  no bureaucracy, fast decisions, real feedback

- A role that grows — the goal is for you to fully 

  own the marketing function of this business



---



HOW TO APPLY



We will receive many applications. Most will not be read 

because they do not follow instructions. Be different.



Send your application to 

Subject line: ELITE MARKETING — [Your Name]



Include ALL of the following. Missing any one item 

means your application will not be reviewed.



1. VIDEO INTRODUCTION (required)

   Film yourself speaking to camera for 60 to 90 seconds.

   Tell us: who you are, what you have done, what tools 

   you use, and what excites you about this role.

   Upload to Google Drive and send the link. No video = no response.



2. YOUR BEST VIDEO EDITING WORK (required)

   Send 2 to 3 examples of short-form video you have 

   personally edited. Reels, TikToks, or similar. 

   Show us your best. Links or files accepted.



3. A BUSINESS SOCIAL MEDIA ACCOUNT YOU HAVE MANAGED (required)

   Show us a real business page you ran. Send the handle 

   or screenshots. We want to see strategy, not just 

   pretty posts.



4. ANSWER THESE TWO QUESTIONS (required)

   Keep your answers honest and specific. 

   Do not write generic answers.



   Question 1:

   "Elite Trades Australia is a Melbourne plumbing, 

   electrical, and heat pump installation business. 

   We have no real brand presence right now. 

   If you were starting from scratch, what would 

   you do in the first 30 days and why?"



   Question 2:

   "What is one thing most trades businesses do wrong 

   on social media, and what would you do differently?"



We are serious about finding the right person. 

We are willing to wait for them.

If you are that person — apply now.

title: UI/UX Specialist / CSS Cleanup - Astro & Docker (Project-Based)

title: IT Technical Support Engineer - Permanent WFH Remote, Flexible Hours (+10 GMT)
description:
*** DO NOT APPLY UNTIL YOU HAVE READ THIS AD IN FULL ***



Amigo Technology is an IT service business and Managed Service Provider (MSP), located on the Sunshine Coast, Australia. We have been operating for 16 years, with the goal of making computers and technology friendlier to our awesome small business clients. We are seeking a Technical IT service officer to join our friendly team via a permanent, work-from-home arrangement.



By joining the Amigo team, you will enjoy:



- A flexible work from home position, up to 40 hours per week, on a schedule that works for you

- Unlimited access to our internal virtual lab, mentoring and training programs, where you will get to experience the latest and greatest technologies with a strong emphasis on open source systems

- Plenty of hands-on time with workstations, servers, networking and cybersecurity projects

- A friendly team atmosphere where you’ll need a good sense of humour!

- Daily opportunities to help ordinary people with technology challenges in a team environment, with the goal of making technology friendly

- Competitive earnings, typically between 370php and 475php per hour, depending on ability and experience, plus bonuses



Ready to join? Here’s what you’ll need:



- A keen interest in working with and growing your skill sets through a wide variety of modern computing technologies (both Windows and Linux - bonus points if you have a home lab!)

- A high level of attention to detail (you must put the word "AMIGO" in the “Additional Comments” section of our application form to be considered)

- At least two years of experience working in an IT service position, ideally in an MSP that works with Windows 11, Microsoft 365 OR Google Workspace environments



This position will have many opportunities for growth, and over time you will have to encourage you to work with a wider range of technologies and platforms. Prior experience with the following would be greatly appreciated and will lead to greater earnings, although this is not a requirement:



- Debian-based Linux (eg. Linux Mint 22 / Ubuntu 24.04 or newer)

- Apple MacOS 13+

- Proxmox 8+ & Docker (or other hypervisor / virtualisation platforms)

- Small business networking and storage systems (eg. Opnsense, OpenWrt, Cockpit), including configuring Wi-Fi, DHCP, QOS, VLANs, VPNs, Samba, ZFS RAID, etc

- FreePBX 16+ / Asterisk, IP Phones & SIP



Your duties will include the following:



- Processing technical support requests, enquiries and system alerts

- Providing remote IT services via RMM & IP KVMs

- Assisting with new and existing IT systems, including setting up, migrating, monitoring, documenting and securing technical systems, such as workstations, servers and cloud-based infrastructure



If you have made it this far, great! Before you apply:



- You will be expected to learn quickly and contribute as part of a team. The workload will be both challenging and rewarding.

- We conduct background checks and our team is required to use managed remote workstations. Dishonest conduct will be quickly caught. If you’re thinking of using a mouse jiggler or being OE, please don’t apply.

- Applicants MUST submit their application via our application form, which can be found on the Amigo Technology Sunshine Coast website via the Careers section in the top menu. All other application methods will be IGNORED. 
/form-technical-officer-application



Please double check the responses to your resume to ensure they are correct - over 90% of applicants fail to submit their application with correct answers. We will only contact valid submissions. We look forward to hearing from you!

title: Part-Time Content Creator + Admin Support
description:
About Nokvex



Nokvex is a Philippines-based content studio serving remote agencies and freelancers across Australia and North America. We offer flexible payment in USD or PHP, treat our team as long-term partners, and keep communication clear and respectful.



---



The Role



We're looking for a Part-Time Content Creator with light admin support responsibilities. This is a long-term role with a clear path to full-time.



20 hours/week | $300/month USD ($70/week) | Weekly payment | Starting immediately



---



Schedule



- 20 hours/week distributed across Monday to Friday primarily

- Flexible - you choose your daily hours within PHT business time

- Occasional weekend support (max 2x/month) for urgent content or time-sensitive posts

- Results-based, not hour-based monitoring



---



Main Responsibilities



1. Video Editing (40%)

- 2 long-form YouTube videos per month (8-15 minutes each)

- 8 short-form videos per month (30-60 seconds, Reels/Shorts/TikTok)

- AI education content, cartoon/Nickelodeon-inspired style

- Dynamic captions, B-roll, basic motion graphics



2. Graphic Design (20%)

- YouTube thumbnails

- Social media post graphics (Canva)

- Simple branded assets



3. AI Asset Generation (15%)

- Image generation (Nano Banana, Midjourney, or similar)

- Character art, backgrounds, supporting visuals

- Following detailed briefs



4. Social Media Management (15%)

- Scheduling posts across Instagram,
, TikTok

- Basic caption drafting

- Engagement monitoring



5. Light Admin Support (10%)

- File organization (Google Drive)

- Basic research tasks (competitor analysis, data gathering)

- Spreadsheet updates

- Simple documentation



---



Growth Path



As our content volume grows, this role will evolve:



- Month 4-6: Hours may increase to 30-40 hrs/week, salary $450-$600

- Month 7-12: Option for full-time at $650-$800/month

- Scope will narrow to specialization (video editing + graphic design focus)

- SMM and admin tasks will transfer to other tea
mbers



Salary reviewed every 3 months based on performance.



---



Who You Are



- 1-2+ years experience in video editing (Premiere Pro, CapCut, or DaVinci Resolve)

- Portfolio showing YouTube long-form AND short-form work

- Basic graphic design skills (Canva minimum)

- Familiar with AI image tools (Midjourney, DALL-E, or similar) OR willing to learn fast

- Comfortable with social media scheduling tools

- Good English written communication

- Reliable internet (backup required)

- Available during regular PHT business hours



Bonus Points:

- Experience with cartoon/animation-style editing

- After Effects skills

- Thumbnail design portfolio (YouTube-specific)

- AI education or tech content experience



---



What We Offer



- $300 USD/month ($70/week), paid weekly via Wise

- 20 hours/week with flexible schedule

- Occasional weekend availability for time-sensitive tasks (max 2x/month)

- 13th month bonus after 6 months of continuous service

- 5 days paid time off per year, available after 3 months

- Clear 12-month growth path (part-time ? full-time)

- Long-term collaboration, not a gig

- Respectful, async-first work culture



---



How to Apply



Please send:



1. Your portfolio link (video editing + graphic design samples)

2. Short answer: What's your favorite editing project and why?

3. A 30-second Loom video introducing yourself

4. Your availability (days and PHT hours)



Applications without a portfolio will not be reviewed.



We review applications within 3 days and reply to everyone.



Learn more at

title: Customer Service Specialist (Fashion Dropshipping)
description:
We are looking for a highly dependable, detail-oriented Customer Service with proven experience in fashion dropshipping. Who thrives in a fast-paced environment, works independently, and consistently delivers accurate, high-quality results. This role plays a critical part in ensuring smooth day-to-day operations—from customer communication to supplier coordination and order fulfillment. Many customer interactions will be supported by AI tools, so efficiency and adaptability are key.



Key Responsibilities

• Manage daily workflows using internal spreadsheets and operational tools, ensuring all records are accurate and up to date

• Respond to HelpScout tickets efficiently (target: 30–40 tickets per hour) while maintaining high-quality support

• Handle return requests in line with company policy, including offering resolutions such as discounts, refunds, or return instructions

• Report damaged, incorrect, or missing items to suppliers and secure timely replacements or refunds

• Coordinate closely with suppliers on shipment updates, fulfillment issues, cancellations, and inventory concerns

• Monitor and follow up on all unfulfilled Shopify orders, resolving issues related to sizing, color, (add the word "sky" on the subject line when applying) stock availability, and address verification

• Collaborate cross-functionally with Customer Service and Supply Chain teams to ensure seamless operations



Qualifications

• Required: Proven experience in fashion dropshipping and order fulfillment (applications without this will not be considered)

• Strong hands-on experience with CJ Dropshipping, especially in order processing and supplier coordination

• Experience using platforms such as Shopify, HelpScout, Zendesk, Gorgias, or similar tools

• Excellent written and verbal communication skills

• Strong organizational skills with the ability to manage multiple tasks independently

• Detail-oriented with strong problem-solving and analytical abilities

• Proactive mindset with the confidence to suggest improvements to workflows and processes



What We Offer

• Competitive salary + performance-based (KPI) bonuses

• Fully remote, long-term position

• Flexible schedule within agreed working hours

• Opportunities for growth and advancement as the company scales



How to Apply - please submit the following:

• Your CV/Resume

• In under 1-minute provide a Loom video explaining how you organize your day

• A cover letter outlining your experience in fashion dropshipping, supplier coordination, and order fulfillment, and why you’re a strong fit for this role

title: Media Buyer and Creative Strategist Meta
description:
We are looking for an experienced Meta Media Buyer and Creative Strategist who wants to take full ownership, build a proper system, and lead the paid media of a growing premium ecommerce brand.

 

About the Role

 

Our brand sells a premium natural product on Meta 
 and Instagram). We have tested creative, gathered data, and learned a lot, but our systems are still hit and miss. We are looking for someone who brings their own proven approach, takes the lead, and builds something stronger from the ground up.

 

You will own Meta paid media strategy, customer persona research, creative angle development, testing structure, cutting and scaling decisions, and weekly reporting. You will also communicate regularly and walk us through your decisions clearly.

 

Who We Are Looking For

 

Proven track record scaling ecommerce brands profitably on Meta.

 

Your own performance tracking and creative testing system. Please be ready to share a sample or redacted example.

 

Clear, repeatable cutting and scaling logic.

 

Comfortable with AI and creative tools such as ChatGPT, Claude, Canva, CapCut, Midjourney, Runway, Triple Whale, Motion, Foreplay, or similar. You are welcome to use any tools you feel comfortable with and able to use effectively.

 

Strong written and spoken English.

 

Autonomous, reliable, and able to lead rather than wait for instructions.

 

Engagement and Hours

 

We are looking for an intermediate to senior level Media Buyer and Creative Strategist. Full time remote is preferred, though we are open to starting part time with the right candidate and a path to full time as the working relationship develops. Expected hours are between 20 and 40 per week depending on engagement and preference. Please propose the rate that works best for you based on your experience.

 

How to Apply

 

1. Start your message with the word TEAK so we know you read this in full.

 

2. Answer briefly. Short, direct answers are welcome.

 

   a. Which brands have you scaled profitably on Meta and what was the ROAS trajectory.

 

   b. What does your first 30 days working with an ecommerce brand look like.

 

   c. Do you work from your own performance tracking and creative testing spreadsheets. Please share a sample or redacted example if possible.

 

   d. Are you willing to regularly communicate and comfortable walking us through your decisions, reasoning, and changes as we go.

 

3. Propose your preferred engagement type and rate.

 

We are more interested in how you think than how you pitch.

 

If the fit feels right on both sides, we will move forward with a short intro call and further communication.

title: US Tax Strategist – Small Business Tax Planning (Full-Time, Remote)
description:
Salary: $2,400 – $3,200/month USD (based on experience)

Hours: Full-time, 40 hours/week

Schedule: Monday - Friday 8am-5pm US Central Time

Start: As soon as the right person is found



About Us

Sin City CFO is a US-based tax advisory firm that helps online business owners — coaches, consultants, agencies, and creators — legally reduce their tax burden through proactive, year-round planning.

We don't file tax returns. We build tax strategies, implement them, and track every dollar saved. A partner CPA/EA firm handles the filing. We handle everything that makes the filing matter.

This is a stable, long-term role with a growing firm. We're looking for someone who wants to build something — not just fill a seat.



What This Role Is

You will own the backend tax planning operations for our client portfolio. That means building strategy plans, implementing and tracking strategies, and maintaining a clear savings report card for every client so they always know exactly what their tax plan is worth.

You will not meet directly with clients. But your work is what our advisors present and stand behind in every client conversation.

This role is right for you if you take pride in building systems, staying ahead of deadlines, and solving problems before someone has to ask.



Your Responsibilities

- Build and maintain a personalized tax strategy plan for each client

- Implement strategies and track projected vs. actual tax savings throughout the year

- Maintain a tax savings report card per client — clear, current, and auditable

- Identify new planning opportunities as client situations evolve

- Build and document repeatable workflows so the work runs consistently without constant direction



US Tax Strategies You Must Know

- S-Corporation election and reasonable compensation planning

- Qualified Business Income (QBI) deduction optimization

- Augusta Rule (Section 280A)

- SEP IRA, Solo 401(k), and defined benefit retirement plan structuring

- Entity structuring for tax efficiency

- Cost segregation (conceptual working knowledge)



Experience with US small business taxation is required. This is not a bookkeeping or return preparation role — it is a planning and strategy role.



You're a Great Fit If You:

- Have hands-on experience implementing US tax strategies for small business owners

- Can build a tax plan from a client's income, entity, and goals — without needing step-by-step direction

- Naturally spot what's missing in a plan, not just what was asked for

- Build checklists and workflows so your work is consistent and doesn't rely on memory

- Write clearly and communicate proactively — this is a fully remote, async role

- Are looking for a long-term role where you can grow with a firm, not just complete a project



About Working With Us

- Full-time, fully remote

- US business hours  (Central Time Zone)

- Long-term position — we're building a team, not filling a short-term gap

- You'll have clear ownership of your work and room to build systems your way

title: DM Appointment Setter
description:
We run a fast-growing results-driven online fitness coaching company helping busy Asian men lose stubborn belly fat, build lean muscle, and stay in shape without cutting out the cultural foods they love.



Our brand and client base is growing fast and we have TOO MANY leads to handle. As a result, we are rapidly expanding our team and looking for a high-performance DM setter who knows how to turn conversations into booked calls so we can help even more people!



If you thrive in a results-driven environment and want uncapped earning potential, keep reading.



Compensation:



- Full-time: 40–50 hours per week

- Pay: $650 USD per month

- Commission: 3-5% of cash collected from every client you book who enrolls

- Expected Earnings: $800–$1,200+ per month



**Top performers earn significantly more. This is a performance-based role. Your income scales with your output.



What You’ll Be Responsible For:



- Initiating conversations with new followers, story viewers, and engaged prospects

- Reaching out to warm and targeted outbound leads daily

- Using our scripts + frameworks while making conversations feel natural and human

- Asking intelligent qualifying questions

- Moving qualified prospects into booked sales calls

- Following up consistently with warm leads

- Tracking conversations and outcomes inside our CRM

- Maintaining our brand voice and communication standards

- You will be expected to generate volume while maintaining quality.



What We’re Looking For:



- Strong written English (clear, natural, professional — no grammar mistakes)

- Quick response speed and ability to handle multiple conversations at once

- 1–3+ years of experience as a DM setter (fitness industry preferred)

- Proven history of booking qualified calls that actually show up

- Reliable laptop + fast internet

- Comfortable sending 80–100+ high-quality messages per day

- Willing to work flexible hours, including weekends when needed

- Highly coachable and able to take feedback fast

- Competitive, disciplined, and motivated by performance

- We move quickly. You must be comfortable keeping up.



Why This Is a Big Opportunity:



- Work directly with a rapidly scaling online fitness coaching company with a passion for client results and excellence across the board

- Clear SOPs, scripts, and leadership support

- Real growth path for top performers (raises + leadership opportunities)

- Paid onboarding and training

title: U.S. Enrolled Agent (EA)
description:
About the Role:

We are seeking an experienced U.S. Enrolled Agent (EA) to support a growing U.S.-based accounting firm. This role is ideal for a tax professional with strong technical expertise in U.S. federal and state taxation who is comfortable working remotely while collaborating closely with a U.S. team.



You will play a key role in tax preparation, compliance, IRS correspondence, and client support during tax season and throughout the year.



Key Responsibilities:

Prepare and review U.S. federal and state tax returns for:

Individuals (Form 1040)

Partnerships (Form 1065)

Corporations (Form 1120 / 1120S)

Trusts and Estates (Form 1041, if applicable)

Analyze financial statements and trial balances for tax preparation

Perform tax research and document findings

Respond to IRS notices and draft professional responses

Assist with tax planning and projections

Support audit representation and IRS communications when required

Ensure accuracy, completeness, and compliance with U.S. tax laws

Communicate with U.S.-based clients and internal staff professionally and clearly

Maintain organized digital workpapers and documentation



Qualifications:

Active Enrolled Agent (EA) credential issued by the IRS

Minimum 3+ years of hands-on U.S. tax preparation experience

Strong knowledge of federal and multi-state taxation

Experience working with U.S. accounting firms (preferred)

Proficiency in U.S. tax software such as:

Lacerte

UltraTax

ProSeries

Drake

CCH (ProSystem fx)

Familiarity with QuickBooks Online/Desktop and financial reporting

Strong English communication skills (written and verbal)

Ability to work during U.S. business hours

Reliable internet connection and dedicated home office setup



Preferred Experience:

Experience supporting high-volume tax seasons

Exposure to U.S. small business clients

Knowledge of bookkeeping and clean-up work

Familiarity with practice management systems (Karbon, Canopy, TaxDome, etc.)

The Role:



The PPC Specialist plays a hands-on role in the day-to-day execution of Amazon advertising campaigns. You’ll be responsible for uploading bulk files, adjusting bids and budgets, building campaigns, harvesting keywords, and ensuring PPC performance stays aligned with ACoS, TACoS, and budget targets. - Adjust bids, update budgets, and modify placement settings

- Track and maintain campaign health daily to ensure accounts remain within budget

title: Media Buyer (META)
description:
Responsibilities



Launch
+ ads per week on
 ads manager

Comfortable working with multiple ad formats: video, static image, native, and long-form/listicle text ads

Experience setting up different link structures, landing page variations, and tracking setups

Detail-oriented — at 200+ ads/week, small mistakes compound fast

Reliable and able to commit to a consistent 3-day-per-week schedule

Ability to work independently with minimal hand-holding



Nice to Have



Experience with direct response / performance marketing

Familiarity with creative testing frameworks

Experience with bulk sheet uploads and campaign automation tools



To Apply

Your experience with Meta Ads Manager (years + types of accounts)

The highest weekly ad launch volume you've handled

A quick note on how you'd approach launching 200 ads in a week without losing quality

title: Rockstar Cold Caller (Lead Gen & Outreach)
description:
I am looking to hire 2–3 high-performing Cold Callers to join our team for lead generation and outbound outreach.



This is not a beginner role. We need professionals who are comfortable navigating gatekeepers, handling objections, and closing for the appointment. If you are confident on the phone and know how to turn a "maybe" into a booked opportunity, we want to talk to you.



Position Details

Compensation: Competitive Hourly Rate

Growth: Rate increases based on performance, consistency, and KPIs

Environment: 100% Flexible Remote Work

Schedule: Must be available to work during US Business Hours (EST/PST)



What You’ll Do

Outbound Prospecting: Make high-volume cold calls to potential leads.

Engagement: Build immediate rapport and clearly articulate our value proposition.

Lead Qualification: Filter prospects and set qualified appointments.

Administrative Tracking: Keep activity logs updated in Google Sheets or our CRM.

Nurturing: Send professional follow-up
s to prospects after calls.

Reporting: Provide brief daily updates on call volume and outcomes.

Scripts and training materials are provided, but you must be able to think on your feet during live conversations.



What I’m Looking For

Proven Track Record: Solid experience in cold calling (Real Estate, SaaS, or Insurance experience is a plus).

Flawless Communication: Strong English verbal and written skills.

Resilience: A "thick skin" and a positive attitude toward handling rejection.

Reliability: High-speed internet, a quiet workspace, and a proven history of punctuality.

Data-Driven: Ability to meet daily/weekly KPIs and report data accurately.

Coachability: Open to feedback and willing to refine your pitch based on results.



How to Apply (Read Carefully)

To ensure you have great attention to detail (a must for this job), please follow these steps exactly:

Subject Line: Use the word “PINK” in your application title.

Voice Recording: Attach or link a 1-minute voice recording introducing yourself and highlighting your cold calling wins.

The "Why": Briefly explain what makes you more effective on the phone than other callers.



Note: Applications that do not follow these instructions or are missing a voice sample will be automatically disqualified.

title: $8,000 From ONE Partnership - Need Crypto Network, Not Experience (Remote BD)
description:
Job Description:

We’re a DeFi protocol launching game-changing LP bond technology. We need Business Development partners who can introduce us to crypto launchpads, blockchain projects, and DeFi communities.





THE OPPORTUNITY:

Every successful introduction that leads to partnership earns you serious money. One good partnership could net you $5,000-10,000 in your first month.





COMPENSATION:

Monetize your network through three massive income streams: guaranteed ETH salary, instant token unlocks, and recurring commissions. We pay you to open doors you’ve already built, while we handle 100% of the closing.



Guaranteed Base Pay: You will receive $10/hour (capped at 10 hours/week),       for simply activating your network. 50% paid in Ethereum (ETH) for immediate cash and 50% in $GMI tokens, which are subject to an 18-month vesting schedule, building a long-term position in the $GMI mutli-chain ecosystem.



Performance Bonus: Every 5 successful partnership you close triggers an immediate unlock of 20% your vested tokens. This allows you to bypass the 18-month vesting period and access your earnings as soon as you deliver results.

Note: A "successful partnership" is defined by the project signing the LP Bonds integration agreement and going live on our marketplace.



Additional Commission: 

- 25% commission on ALL upgrade fees from your partnerships

- Level 2 upgrades: $40 upgrade fee, you get $10 each

- Level 3 upgrades: $80 upgrade fee, you get $20 each

- Level 4 upgrades: $160 upgrade fee, you get $40 each



-  $500 bonus when your partnerships hit 25 Level 4 bonds

-  $2,000 bonus at 100 Level 4 bonds

-  $5,000 bonus at 300 Level 4 bonds

-  Realistic month 1 earnings: $2,000-5,000

- Total 90-day potential: $20,000+



YOU HAVE:

- Existing connections in crypto (launchpads, DAOs, blockchain projects)

-
/
 relationships with project founders or marketing heads

- Understanding of DeFi/crypto space

- Hunger to earn based on results, not hours





YOU DON’T NEED:

- To close deals (I handle that)

- Any upfront investment

- Previous BD experience (connections matter more)

- Full-time commitment (work your own schedule)





THE ROLE:

Simply introduce me to decision-makers at crypto projects. I close the deals, you collect commissions. Every user from your partnerships who upgrades their bonds puts money in your pocket..

Example: ONE medium-sized launchpad partnership generating 100 L4 bonds = $8,000 for you.



Ready to turn your crypto connections into serious income?



Apply with:

- List 3-5 crypto projects/launchpads you have connections with

- Your
 handle

- Brief description of your crypto involvement



We need partners who see the opportunity and want to run with it. Start earning within days of your first successful introduction!

title: Senior Full-Stack Developer (DeFi and NFT Projects)
description:
About Us

LP Bonds. Live DeFi protocol. 8+ chains. 50+ pools. Real users. Real TVL. We built this with a team most startups would call "impossible." We're not looking for someone who's done 10 years of safe corporate blockchain work. We're looking for someone who's hungry enough to own an entire protocol's technical stack and sharp enough to not break it.



THE ROLE

 You own everything technical. Smart contracts. Frontend. Backend. New chain deployments. DEX integrations. Infrastructure. There's no senior engineer above you to review your code. There's no architect drawing diagrams for you. You are the engineering department.



WHAT YOU'LL DO:

Solidity & Contracts:

- Deploy and manage production smart contracts across EVM chains - Bond NFTs, LP vaults, yield engines, upgrade systems, marketplace

- Ship new chain deployments end-to-end. We hand you a chain, you hand us live contracts.

- Integrate with DEX protocols (Camelot, PancakeSwap, Uniswap) at the contract level

- Security is your religion. You think about how things break before you think about how they work.



Frontend:

- React/Next.js - build and maintain the full user-facing application

- Web3 wallet integrations, stats dashboard, Bond NFT marketplace

- If the UI is broken, it's your problem. If it's slow, it's your problem.



Backend & Infra:

- Node.js API, MongoDB, IPFS

- Multi-chain indexing and event monitoring

- Keep 8+ chain deployments healthy simultaneously



WHO YOU ARE:

- You've written Solidity that's been deployed to mainnet. Even once. Even small. You've felt the weight of real money in your contract.

- You can build a full-stack app alone. Not "I contributed to a team project" - you built it.

- You learn by doing, not by asking. You hit a wall, you Google it, you read the docs, you figure it out, you ship.

- You're not waiting for permission. You see something broken, you fix it. You see something missing, you build it.

- You're young, hungry, and slightly terrified of how much responsibility this is. Good. That fear keeps you sharp.





WE CARE ABOUT:

- What you've shipped. Show us. Links or it didn't happen.

- How fast you learn when thrown into something you've never done

- Whether you take ownership or make excuses

- Whether you stay up until 3am because the bug is bothering you, not because someone told you to



This is a small team building something most of DeFi hasn't figured out yet. You will be stretched. You will ship things you didn't think you could ship. You will learn more here than anywhere else because there's no one to hide behind.



If that excites you, apply. If it scares you, even better - apply anyway.

title: Health Insurance Data and Contracting Support
description:
ABOUT US

We are a growing health insurance agency dedicated to helping individuals and families across the United States find the right health coverage. Our team operates in a fast-paced, remote-first environment where every tea
mber plays a vital role in delivering exceptional service. We are expanding and looking for a talented Virtual Assistant to join our team and grow with us.

WHAT WE OFFER

Competitive hourly pay of $3–$7/hour based on experience, plus commission opportunities.

A clear path for growth — top performers will have opportunities for increased pay, expanded responsibilities, and leadership roles.

Fully remote position — work from anywhere with a reliable internet connection.

A supportive, team-oriented culture where your contributions are valued and recognized.

Hands-on training and exposure to the U.S. health insurance industry, building valuable long-term skills.

POSITION OVERVIEW

We are seeking a detail-oriented Data & Contracting Specialist to manage the backbone operations of our agency. This role is critical to keeping our agent contracting, data fulfillment, invoicing, and administrative systems running smoothly. You will support the agency’s key players by handling essential back-office functions that directly impact growth and profitability.

KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

Manage the full agent contracting lifecycle: process new agent appointments, carrier applications, certifications, and onboarding documentation.

Track and maintain contracting status for all agents across multiple carriers, ensuring timely renewals, compliance, and up-to-date records.

Execute data fulfillment processes including lead data management, list preparation, data segmentation, and delivery to marketing partners.

Manage invoicing for the team and coordinate billing with the agency’s data and marketing company partners.

Maintain and update agency databases, spreadsheets, and CRM records with accuracy and consistency.

Provide administrative support to agency leadership, including scheduling, document preparation, and task management.

Prepare reports on contracting pipeline, data delivery metrics, and financial summaries as requested.

Coordinate with carriers, marketing vendors, and internal tea
mbers to resolve data discrepancies and contracting issues.

REQUIRED QUALIFICATIONS

Exceptional attention to detail and organizational skills — you thrive on accuracy and process management.

Strong computer proficiency including advanced skills in spreadsheets (Excel/Google Sheets), CRM platforms, and data management tools.

Experience with invoicing, billing, or basic bookkeeping processes.

Minimum 1–2 years of experience in an administrative, data entry, or operations support role.

Ability to manage multiple priorities and deadlines independently with minimal supervision.

Strong written communication skills for professional correspondence with carriers, vendors, and tea
mbers.

Reliable high-speed internet connection and a dedicated, distraction-free workspace.

PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS

Previous experience in health insurance agent contracting or insurance agency operations.

Familiarity with GoHighLevel (GHL) or similar CRM platforms.

Experience working with carrier contracting portals and appointment systems.

Background in data fulfillment, lead management, or marketing operations.

Experience with invoicing tools such as QuickBooks, FreshBooks, or similar platforms.



HOW TO APPLY

Send your resume along with a brief cover message explaining why you are the right fit for this role. Please include:

• Your relevant experience  • Your availability and time zone  • Your internet speed test results

description:

Monitor and respond to inbound text replies and campaign responses promptly to maximize conversion rates.

Maintain organized records of all prospect interactions, appointment outcomes, and follow-up tasks in the CRM.

Collaborate with the sales team to refine messaging, scripts, and targeting strategies based on performance data.

Meet or exceed daily and weekly targets for outbound activity (calls, texts, and appointments set).

Manage and clean prospect lists, flag bad data, and ensure campaign compliance with TCPA and messaging regulations.

REQUIRED QUALIFICATIONS

Strong computer skills with the ability to manage multiple software tools, browser tabs, and workflows simultaneously.

Comfortable making high-volume outbound calls and engaging prospects in a professional and persuasive manner.

Excellent written communication skills — must be able to craft clear, concise, and engaging text messages.

Self-motivated with a strong work ethic and a results-oriented mindset.

Minimum 1 year of experience in outbound sales, telemarketing, appointment setting, or lead generation.

Reliable high-speed internet connection, a quiet workspace, and a professional-quality headset.

PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS

Previous experience in the health insurance or insurance industry.

Familiarity with GoHighLevel (GHL), including workflows, automations, and campaign management.

Experience with dialers, SMS platforms, or outreach automation tools (e.g., PhoneBurner, Mojo, RingCentral).

Understanding of TCPA compliance and text messaging best practices.

Proven track record of meeting or exceeding appointment-setting or sales quotas.



HOW TO APPLY

Send your resume along with a brief cover message explaining why you are the right fit for this role. Please include:

• Your relevant experience  • Your availability and time zone  • Your internet speed test results

title: Health Insurance Customer Support Specialist
description:
NOW HIRING

Client Support Specialist

Health Insurance Agency  |  Remote  |  Full-Time VA  |  $4–$8/hr + Commission opportunities





ABOUT US

We are a growing health insurance agency dedicated to helping individuals and families across the United States find the right health coverage. Our team operates in a fast-paced, remote-first environment where every tea
mber plays a vital role in delivering exceptional service. We are expanding and looking for a talented Virtual Assistant to join our team and grow with us.

WHAT WE OFFER

Competitive hourly pay of $4–$8/hour based on experience, plus commission opportunities.

A clear path for growth — top performers will have opportunities for increased pay, expanded responsibilities, and leadership roles.

Fully remote position — work from anywhere with a reliable internet connection.

A supportive, team-oriented culture where your contributions are valued and recognized.

Hands-on training and exposure to the U.S. health insurance industry, building valuable long-term skills.



POSITION OVERVIEW

We are looking for a highly skilled Client Support Specialist to serve as the primary point of contact for our clients after the sale is made. You will be the face and voice of the agency, guiding policyholders through their health insurance benefits, resolving issues, and ensuring an exceptional client experience. This role requires outstanding English communication skills, a professional phone presence, and the ability to navigate the complex U.S. health insurance landscape with confidence.



KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

Serve as the main point of contact for all post-sale client inquiries via phone,
, and messaging platforms.

Guide clients through their health insurance plans, including coverage details, claims processes, provider networks, and benefit explanations.

Assist clients with enrollment changes, plan modifications, qualifying life events, and annual renewal support.

Troubleshoot and resolve billing discrepancies, claims issues, and coverage questions by coordinating with carriers and internal tea
mbers.

Maintain detailed and accurate records of all client interactions in the agency CRM (GoHighLevel/GHL).

Proactively follow up with clients to ensure satisfaction, policy understanding, and retention.

Escalate complex cases to senior tea
mbers or carriers as needed while keeping clients informed throughout the process.

Stay current on health insurance regulations, ACA guidelines, carrier updates, and plan changes that may impact clients.



REQUIRED QUALIFICATIONS

Impeccable spoken and written English — must sound natural, professional, and confident on live calls with U.S.-based clients.

Strong understanding of the U.S. health insurance industry, including ACA/Marketplace plans, Medicare, Medicaid, short-term health, and ancillary products.

Exceptional computer skills with the ability to quickly learn and navigate multiple software platforms simultaneously.

Minimum 1–2 years of experience in a client-facing support role, preferably in the insurance or healthcare industry.

Outstanding problem-solving skills with the ability to handle difficult client situations with professionalism and empathy.

Reliable high-speed internet connection, a quiet workspace, and professional-quality headset for calls.



PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS

Previous experience working in a U.S. health insurance agency or brokerage.

Familiarity with GoHighLevel (GHL) or similar CRM platforms.

Experience with carrier portals such as 
, SHOP, or direct carrier enrollment systems.

Licensed or willing to pursue health insurance licensing (state-specific).



HOW TO APPLY

Send your resume along with a brief cover message explaining why you are the right fit for this role. Please include:

• Your relevant experience  • Your availability and time zone  • Your internet speed test results





We are an equal opportunity employer. We celebrate diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all tea
mbers.

title: Patient Care Coordinator
description:
We are looking for a highly motivated and experienced Remote Patient Services Representative based in the Philippines. The ideal candidate will manage inbound and outbound calls, assist patients with scheduling appointments, address inquiries, and perform other related tasks to ensure a seamless patient experience.



Key Responsibilities:

• Answer incoming calls promptly and professionally.

• Schedule, reschedule, and confirm patient appointments using our electronic health record (EHR) system.

• Provide accurate information regarding services, insurance, and payment options.

• Address patient inquiries and concerns with empathy and efficiency.

• Maintain and update patient records with a high degree of accuracy.

• Collaborate with the optometry team to ensure optimal patient care.



Performance Metrics:

To ensure excellence in patient service, the following performance metrics will be used to evaluate success in this role:

• Average Handle Time (AHT): The average duration of a patient interaction, including talk time, hold time, and after-call work. Efficiently managing AHT ensures timely assistance to patients while maintaining service quality. ?

• Quality Score: Assessment of call quality based on adherence to protocols, accuracy of information provided, and overall patient interaction effectiveness. High-quality scores reflect a commitment to excellence in patient care.

• Patient Satisfaction Score: A measure of patient contentment with the service provided, often gathered through post-interaction surveys. High scores indicate successful patient engagement and issue resolution.

• Service Level: The percentage of calls answered within a specified timeframe, reflecting the ability to meet patient demand promptly. Maintaining a high service level ensures patient satisfaction and loyalty. ?

• Appointment Scheduling Rate: The efficiency and accuracy in scheduling patient appointments, ensuring optimal utilization of clinic time and resources.



Qualifications:

• Proven experience in a customer service role, preferably in a medical or optometry setting.

• Exceptional verbal and written English communication skills.

• Familiarity with EHR systems and medical scheduling software is a plus.

• Strong organizational skills and attention to detail.

• Ability to work independently and manage time effectively.

• Stable internet connection and a quiet workspace conducive to professional phone conversations.



Work Schedule:

• Full-time position.

• Must be available to work U.S. business hours, including potential evening shifts to accommodate different time zones.



To Qualify, please complete the Assessment below:



1. Application form: wkf.ms/45nfpCJ

2. Complete this assessment: 
/link/index/JB-BYUGHNDRC?u=

description:
(DO NOT
 US YOUR RESUME. WE ONLY CONSIDER CANDIDATES WHO FILL OUT OUR APPLICATION BELOW)



About the Role:



If you're someone who loves social media marketing, gets just as excited about strategy as you do building out content, and wants to take ownership of a key marketing channel in a growing online business, read on...



Learn Jazz Standards is a thriving 7-figure online music education business helping adult musicians finally learn to play jazz without the overwhelm. We're a small team, and that allows us to work fast. We've built a healthy content ecosystem across YouTube, blog, podcast, and
 — and now we're ready to make social media a serious, measurable growth channel.



We're looking for a Social Media Marketer who can do it all: build and execute a strategy, manage the content calendar, create and repurpose content, run ManyChat funnels, and ultimately turn Instagram and
 into real lead generation and sales engines for our business. This role sits at the intersection of execution (posting, scheduling, replying to comments) and strategic marketing (lead gen funnels, selling in the DMs, data driven decision making).



This is a full-time remote role reporting directly to the Founder. You'll be given strategic direction and goals, but we expect you to bring your own ideas, initiatives, and creative thinking to the table.



What You'll Own:



Content Strategy & Execution:



Dramatically increase our content output across Instagram and
 — both in volume and quality

Develop and manage a content calendar using tools like Meta Business Suite or Metricool

Repurpose our deep content library (YouTube videos, course clips, live Q&As, podcast transcripts, blog posts) into platform-native content: Reels, Stories, carousels, static graphics, and more

Write compelling captions, select strategic hashtags, and publish content consistently

Respond to and manage post comments, and interact with leads in the DMs



Content Creation:



Create graphics and short-form video edits when needed (basic video editing required — we have a video editor for heavier lifts, but you need to be able to move fast independently)

Work within our existing brand assets while having the skill to build new templates in Canva or similar tools when the 
nt calls for it



Lead Generation & Sales (Our Primary Objective):



Build and manage ManyChat funnels to convert social media engagement into
 subscribers and customers

Create automated DM flows that generate leads and nurture toward sales

Develop lead magnets from our existing content library to drive opt-ins

Think like a marketer first: every piece of content should serve a lead generation or sales objective



Strategy & Analytics:



Analyze platform data to understand what's working, what's not, and why

Bring data-informed recommendations to weekly strategy sessions with the Founder

Proactively identify new opportunities for growing reach, leads, and sales

Build repeatable systems and document your processes so nothing lives only in your head



What the First 90 Days Look Like:



By the end of your first 90 days, we expect you to have:



Built out a new and improved social media strategy alongside the Founder

Taken full ownership of the content calendar and posting schedule

Meaningfully increased our content output across Instagram and

Launched at least one ManyChat funnel driving measurable leads

Established a reporting rhythm with clear KPIs and progress updates

Demonstrated early, measurable progress — in leads, engagement, or both



What Makes You a Great Fit:



Must-Haves:



-3+ years of experience managing social media for a brand with a lead generation or sales focus — not just vanity metrics

Demonstrated ability to build ManyChat (or equivalent) funnels from scratch — chat automations, DM sequences, lead gen flows



-Strong content creation skills: you can write a great caption, design a clean graphic, and do basic video editing without needing hand-holding



-Comfortable with analytics — you know how to read the data and make decisions from it

Systems-oriented: you build repeatable processes, document your work, and don't let things fall through the cracks



-Self-starter who takes initiative rather than waiting to be told what to do



-Proficient with tools like Meta Business Suite, Metricool (or similar), Canva, and AI tools for content ideation and creation



Strong Plusses:



-Experience with content repurposing workflows — turning long-form content (video, podcast, blog) into social-native formats



-Background in
 marketing or digital funnels (understanding the broader customer journey matters here)



-Genuine enthusiasm for or knowledge of music — especially jazz or music education. Our audience is adult musicians, and understanding how they think, what they struggle with, and the language they use will make everything you do more effective



Compensation & Structure:



Type: Full-time, remote



Salary: $1,500/month (1,075,905 PHP/year)



13th Month Pay: paid out in December



End of year performance bonuses: paid out in December if applicable



Reports to: Founder



Support: You'll have access to a video editor for repurposing existing content, Founder guidance, and support from other tea
mbers when needed.



TO APPLY FILL OUT THIS SHORT APPLICATION:



(DO NOT
 US YOUR RESUME. WE ONLY CONSIDER CANDIDATES WHO FILL OUT OUR APPLICATION)

title: Claude AI Specialist — Part-Time Ongoing
description:
Ongoing part-time role maintaining and improving our Claude AI setup (Cowork, skills, scheduled tasks, MCP). Looking for a tidy, technical specialist with strong English.



ABOUT US



Tama Group is a property development and tenant advisory business. We're based in Indonesia but operate in English. The founder uses Claude (Anthropic's AI system) as the engine behind a lot of daily work — drafting, research, workflow automation, document review, meeting prep.



Over the past several months we've built out a substantial Claude setup: Cowork workspace, custom skills, scheduled tasks, MCP connectors, and a memory system. It's powerful, but it's getting messy. Skills overlap, automations accumulate, memory gets stale, and nobody owns the hygiene.



That's the role.



THE ROLE



You'll be the person who keeps our Claude operating system clean, well-documented, and continuously improving. This is NOT a one-off build project — it's an ongoing relationship. We're looking for someone who thinks in systems, documents their work, and is allergic to sprawl.



Typical monthly work includes:



1. MAINTENANCE (ongoing)

   - Audit what's running — scheduled tasks, skills, connectors, memory files

   - Prune anything stale, redundant, or broken

   - Keep naming conventions and documentation consistent

   - Maintain a change log so we always know what's changed and why



2. IMPROVEMENT (as needs emerge)

   - Build new skills and automations for specific workflows (tenant communications, deal review, market monitoring, meeting prep, etc.)

   - Improve existing automations based on how they're actually used

   - Set up new MCP connectors as we add tools



3. STEWARDSHIP (continuous)

   - Stay current with Claude's capabilities and flag new features worth adopting

   - Recommend small improvements proactively

   - Respond to questions about our setup when asked



REQUIREMENTS — MUST HAVE



- Hands-on experience with Claude specifically. Not "ChatGPT and AI in general" — Claude. You should know what Cowork, skills, MCP servers, the Agent SDK, and scheduled tasks are, and ideally have built with them. If you have strong AI automation fundamentals and are new to some of these specific Claude features, tell us — we're open to the right person who learns fast.

- Strong written English — clear, concise, professional. You'll be documenting systems and writing briefs.

- Organized, systematic way of working. You keep your own work tidy.

- Ability to explain technical work in plain language.

- At least one example of a Claude or AI automation setup you've built and then maintained over time (not just a one-off demo).



NICE TO HAVE



- Experience with Python or JavaScript (for custom MCP servers and more complex skills)

- Familiarity with n8n, Make, or Zapier

- Background in ops, IT, or technical support roles

- Interest in property / real estate / small business operations



WORKING ARRANGEMENT



- Part-time, starting at approximately 15 hours per week. Rate is $15–$20/hr depending on experience.

- Async-first. Weekly check-in via chat or video.

- Timezone flexible — we're in Indonesia (WIB/UTC+7).

- Paid monthly via OnlineJobs.ph's standard methods.

- 30-day trial period, then open-ended ongoing.



TO APPLY



Please reply with the following, in this order:



1. The word "PRISTINE" at the top of your message (so I know you read the post).

2. 3–5 sentences on your Claude experience. One specific example of a setup or automation you built AND maintained over time. Links or screenshots welcome.

3. How you typically structure an ongoing retainer role — weekly rhythm, how you document work, how you keep a client informed.

4. Your proposed monthly rate and hours.

5. A short note on why this role appeals to you specifically.



I read every application carefully. Generic or templated applications will be ignored.



Thanks for reading — looking forward to hearing from you.

title: Sales & Tech Representative
description:
We’re Hiring!



Are you a skilled salesperson with a background in gas plumbing and/or electrical work? Do you enjoy helping customers find the right solution while using your technical knowledge? Join our growing team and work with a US online home goods retailer that values expertise, professionalism, and customer satisfaction.





Position:

Sales & Tech Representative – Technical Products (Gas & Electrical Focus)

Location: Remote (Philippines-based)

Schedule: Monday-Friday, 11:00 AM to 7:00 PM EST (40 hours/week)

Salary: Competitive + Performance Bonuses





About the Role:

As a Salesperson, you will assist customers with product selection, provide technical support during the sales process, and close sales with confidence. You’ll work with gas fireplaces, heaters, stoves, and other appliances, so previous hands-on experience or technical knowledge of gas or electrical systems is a MUST.





Job Duties and Responsibilities:

Receive incoming phone calls and live chats from potential customers (prospects) who indicated that they need shopping assistance. This does not include calls and chats from existing customers that need help with their orders (that’s a job for a different department)

Help customers choose the right gas or electric heating appliance for their needs

Makes follow-up calls, writes follow-up
s, and text messages to potential buyers. We try to follow up 5x with each person that contacted us (unless they indicate that they are no longer interested). We don’t do cold calling. Only reaching out to prospects who interacted with our online store and left their contact information.

After each interaction with a prospect/customer, writes notes & comments in our CRM (Asana)

Communicate efficiently and build rapport with potential customers

Assists prospects in their shopping journey and helps make the purchase decision

Ensures that all prospects’ questions and objections are addressed

Leads every interaction to a close sale, which means an order is placed on our online store

Prepares custom quotes, price matches competitors if necessary, and offers custom discounts. This requires calculating margins & ensuring profitability.

Answering questions about the products we offer, including technical questions. Learns the specifications, features and benefits of our bestsellers. Over time, becomes an expert in the industry.

Explain technical specifications and installation requirements in simple terms

If not able to answer product-specific questions, conducts research in the materials provided by the supplier, or reaches out to the supplier to get the information

Confirms stock availability and lead times when offering products to prospects

Documents standard procedures and best practices in a written form, so that other tea
mbers can learn from them





Requirements:

Sales Experience – 2+ years, preferably with B2C or B2B technical products

Gas Plumbing or Electrical Experience – Formal training or work experience (installer, technician, or similar roles preferred)

Excellent English – Both written and spoken

Confident Communicator – Can explain complex concepts to non-technical customers

Tech-Savvy – Comfortable using CRMs,
 platforms, order tools, etc.

Reliable and Proactive – Takes initiative and works independently

Home Office Setup – Stable internet, noise-free environment, backup power is a plus





Preferred but Not Required:

Knowledge of venting, HVAC, or hearth appliances

US product or code familiarity

Experience in the construction or home improvement industry





Why Join Us?

Sales commission: 8% of gross profit from all orders closed. Gross profit means the sale amount minus the product and shipping cost. Paid monthly. No upper limit. Monthly commission payouts range between $1,200 (the guaranteed minimum) and as high as $3,000 (for the top performer). The average monthly payout is $1,465 (average from the last 2 years).

$1,200/month minimum guaranteed pay

15 days paid days off per year (also convertible to min. guaranteed commission)

Paid training on all aspects of the role

Opportunities for professional development

Friendly atmosphere

Online Interview process

Fully remote position





How to Apply:

Apply through OnlineJobs.ph with the subject line: "Sales Pro - [Your Name]"

title: Position: Dedicated Biller – Home Care & Financial Management Services
description:
Experienced Home Health Care Biller

Robland Home Health Care & Aspen FMS



Position: Dedicated Biller – Home Health Care & FMS

Employer: Robland Home Health Care / Aspen FMS

Experience Required: Must be already trained and experienced in home health care billing

Work Hours: Monday through Friday, business hours (Central Time)

Work Type: Remote / Virtual

Role Focus: Full-cycle billing under MnDHS guidelines, Medical Assistance (MA), waivered services, and MCOs



About Us

Robland Home Health Care and Aspen FMS provide in-home care and Financial Management Services (FMS) to Minnesotans enrolled in programs administered through the Minnesota Department of Human Services (MnDHS). Accurate, on-time billing is how our caregivers get paid and how the company stays healthy. We’re hiring an experienced biller to take full ownership of this function.

Role Overview

This is not an entry-level or trainee role. We are looking for a biller who already knows home health care billing inside and out — specifically billing under MnDHS guidelines, Medical Assistance (MA), waivered services, and managed care organizations (MCOs). You should be able to hit the ground running: submitting claims in MN–ITS, verifying authorizations, reconciling remittance advices, and working denials without step-by-step hand-holding.

You will own the full billing cycle for both Robland Home Health Care and Aspen FMS. This role reports directly to leadership and works closely with intake, scheduling, and payroll. Because billing accuracy drives revenue and compliance, this is one of the most trusted seats in the company.

Key Responsibilities

Claim Submission

• Submit clean claims to MN–ITS, MA, waivered services, and applicable MCOs on a consistent weekly / bi-weekly cycle.

• Verify that each claim matches the service authorization, documented hours, correct service codes and modifiers, and participant eligibility for the date(s) of service.

• Stay ahead of MnDHS timely-filing deadlines — no missed windows.

• Maintain organized, auditable billing records for every claim submitted.

Authorization & Eligibility

• Verify participant eligibility and active service authorizations before billing.

• Track authorization units, start/end dates, and remaining balances; flag authorizations about to expire or run out.

• Coordinate with intake to ensure new authorizations are captured and entered before services are billed.

Denials, Rejections & AR Follow-up

• Read and reconcile remittance advices (RAs / 835s); identify denials, rejections, and underpayments.

• Diagnose root causes — auth mismatches, documentation gaps, coding issues, eligibility lapses — correct and resubmit within filing windows.

• Maintain a denial log, surface trends, and fix recurring issues at the source rather than re-billing the same problem.

• Keep accounts receivable clean and well-aged; nothing slips past recoverable windows.

Compliance & Documentation

• Ensure all billed services are supported by compliant documentation (timesheets, visit notes, EVV records, care plans) before submission.

• Follow MnDHS billing guidelines, MA policies, and FMS program rules exactly as written.

• Maintain strict HIPAA compliance and confidentiality on all participant, employee, and payer information.

• Pull records quickly for internal or external audits.

Reporting & Communication

• Provide weekly billing reports to leadership: claims submitted, paid, pending, denied, and aging AR.

• Flag revenue risks early — missed windows, repeated denials, documentation gaps, auth issues.

• Communicate clearly with intake, scheduling, and payroll when billing issues trace back to their workflows.

• Respond to MnDHS, MCO, and payer inquiries professionally and on time.

Required Qualifications

• Home Health Care Billing Experience: 2+ years of hands-on billing experience in home health care, home care, PCA, CFSS, waivered services, or a similar Minnesota-based care setting. This is non-negotiable — applicants without direct home care billing experience will not be considered.

• MnDHS / MN–ITS Proficiency: Direct experience submitting claims through MN–ITS under MnDHS guidelines. You know the system, the service codes, and the rules without needing to be taught them.

• Payer Knowledge: Working knowledge of Medical Assistance (MA), waiver programs, and Minnesota managed care organizations (UCare, Blue Plus, Medica, HealthPartners, etc.).

• Denial Management: Proven ability to read 835s / remittance advices, diagnose denials, and rework claims within timely-filing windows.

• EVV Familiarity: Understands Electronic Visit Verification and how EVV data flows into claims.

• Detail-Oriented: Obsessive accuracy with numbers, codes, dates, and documentation. Small errors create large problems in this role.

• Self-Directed: Able to own the billing function independently, set your own cadence, and hit deadlines without being managed minute-to-minute.

• Technology: Comfortable with billing / EHR software, payer portals, Excel / Google Sheets, and payroll coordination.

• Reliability & Discretion: Consistent, on-time performance and full confidentiality on sensitive participant, payroll, and financial information.

• Home Office Setup: Quiet, private workspace; reliable high-speed internet; a working computer; and a dependable backup plan.

Preferred (Nice-to-Have)

• Prior experience billing for an FMS (Financial Management Services) agency.

• CPB, CPC, or other billing / coding certifications.

• Experience supervising or training other billers.

• Experience cleaning up a billing backlog or rebuilding a billing process from the ground up.

What Success Looks Like

• Clean claims go out on time, every cycle — zero missed timely-filing deadlines.

• Denial rate is low and trending down; recurring denial reasons get fixed at the source.

• AR stays healthy and well-aged; nothing quietly slips past recoverable windows.

• Leadership has clear weekly visibility into billing performance and revenue risk.

• Audits are handled calmly because the documentation trail is already clean.

Compensation & Schedule

Schedule: Monday – Friday, standard business hours (Central Time)

Location: Fully remote

Pay: [Rate to be discussed based on experience]

Start Date: As soon as possible for the right candidate

How to Apply

To apply, please submit the following:

• An updated resume or CV specifically highlighting your home health care billing experience — agencies, years, programs billed (MA, waivers, PCA, CFSS, etc.), and systems used (MN–ITS, EHRs, clearinghouses).

• A short cover letter (3–5 sentences) describing the billing setup you’ve owned before and what you’re proud of in your work — clean claim rate, denial turnaround, backlog cleanup, etc.

• Any relevant certifications (CPB, CPC, etc.).

• Your available start date and confirmation that you can commit to a consistent weekday schedule.

Please do not apply if you do not already have direct home health care billing experience. We’re looking for someone who can walk in, plug in, and run the billing function with confidence from day one.



Robland Home Health Care  •  Aspen FMS
